research_question: Can a pre-correction mechanism label predict which correction method repairs a naturally generated title-artist relation conflict?

technical_backup_only.allowed_reason: Confirmatory model fails the format-only technical smoke under both frozen prompt formats.

prompt_templates.primary:
Music playlist request.

User profile: {profile}.

Current need: {current_need}.

Recommend exactly 5 real, existing tracks. For each track, use exactly three fields in this order: Title, Artist, Reason. Keep each reason to one short sentence.

Playlist:
1. Title:
prompt_templates.single_format_fallback:
Music playlist request.

User profile: {profile}.

Current need: {current_need}.

Recommend exactly 5 real, existing tracks. Return exactly five single-line records and no other text. Each record must use this literal form: N. Title: <real title> | Artist: <real artist> | Reason: <one short sentence>

Playlist:
1. Title:

catalog.reference_semantics: catalog-supported reference, not unique real-world answer
archive_requirements: query URL or normalized query parameters

neutral_candidate_exclusions: strict exact artist equals emitted artist
strict exact artist equals reference artist
either catalog lists emitted artist for neutral title
either catalog lists reference artist for neutral title

patch_specification.source: factual-title prompt plus complete candidate artist continuation
patch_specification.target: otherwise identical neutral-title prompt plus the same candidate artist continuation
patch_specification.token_alignment: source and target complete artist continuation token IDs must match
patch_specification.patched_positions: all prediction positions immediately preceding every complete artist continuation token

naive_candidate_free_templates: You previously wrote that the track titled '{title}' is by '{emitted_artist}'. Recheck that factual relation without tools. Output exactly 'Artist: <artist name>' if you can correct or confirm it, or 'ABSTAIN' if you cannot.
Verify the artist for the song title '{title}'. Your earlier answer was '{emitted_artist}'. Do not use tools or a candidate list. Reply only 'Artist: <artist name>' or 'ABSTAIN'.
anti_prior_candidate_free_templates: You previously wrote that the track titled '{title}' is by '{emitted_artist}'. Ignore artist fame and generic name associations; retrieve the specific title-artist relation. Without tools or candidates, reply only 'Artist: <artist name>' or 'ABSTAIN'.

Recall the exact artist linked to the song title '{title}', not the artist who is merely more famous or generally likely. Your earlier answer was '{emitted_artist}'. Reply only 'Artist: <artist name>' or 'ABSTAIN'.
correction.closed_set_manipulation_prompt_template: The artist associated with the track titled {title} is

candidate_free_response_scoring.per_paraphrase_indicator: 1 only when the parsed artist equals the catalog-supported reference after frozen normalization; 0 otherwise
candidate_free_response_scoring.per_title_condition_accuracy: arithmetic mean of the two frozen paraphrase indicators, yielding 0, 0.5, or 1
candidate_free_response_scoring.paraphrase_disagreement: report when the two normalized outputs differ
candidate_free_response_scoring.continued_error: report each parsed non-reference artist separately
candidate_free_response_scoring.invalid: report each response that is neither ABSTAIN nor exactly one Artist field separately
candidate_free_response_scoring.tie_handling: no tie-breaking; the two paraphrase indicators remain separate before averaging

hypothesis_gates.h1_agreement_denominator: all selected normalized-title conflict clusters including indeterminate labels and technical failures

claim_boundaries: No claim that activation patching establishes a unique necessary circuit.
No claim that internal diagnostics replace external catalog grounding.
Recommendation relevance and explanation faithfulness remain separate from entity validity.
